executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 organizations victimized in a single 48-hour period. High-profile targets include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart with database leaked, Peterborough Public Health, and the Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware gang leads the assault with 20+ victims, followed by aggressive campaigns from Play, Clop, and SafePay groups. Critical infrastructure sectors—healthcare, education, municipal government, and financial services—are under sustained attack across all provinces.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20+ Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Waterloo Information Systems, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon), Kuzco Lighting, Traffic Tech, Service Star Freightways, Questica, and multiple law/accounting firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information). Double extortion model with data leak site publication.

items.name: Play
items.targets: 10+ Canadian victims including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products of Canada, Makivik, ALLMAX, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Select Tool, and Pewarchuk CPA
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter). Known for targeting small-to-medium enterprises.

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13+ Canadian organizations including Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, CMH Heli, Ideal Welders, Strategic Objectives, SMT Lawyers, Technikel, and Whiskijack Resorts
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage). Historically exploits zero-day vulnerabilities in file transfer applications.

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 11+ Canadian victims including Teccart College, Briway Carriers, multiple construction firms (Groupe Piche, Kenalex, Silverline Group), and manufacturing (Precision Aluminum)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562 (Impair Defenses), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Phoenix Labs, Esposito Bros Construction, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, CCR Solutions, Emond Publishing, and Soteck
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 4 Canadian victims including Labtician Ophthalmics (healthcare), ControlGMC (manufacturing), Groupe Caddac, and Real Tech
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1003 (OS Credential Dumping), T1021 (Remote Services)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health, KTL Group, Laurysen Kitchens, Group Echo, and Trucash
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562.001 (Disable or Modify Tools), T1018 (Remote System Discovery). Public health targeting indicates critical infrastructure focus.

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 4 Canadian victims including Kids & Company (childcare services), Active Green + Ross, Morison Insurance Brokers, and The Sundher Group
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1136 (Create Account), T1070 (Indicator Removal)

items.name: IncRansom
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Bison Family Medical, Omega Tool Corp, Steelworks Inc, Tract Consulting, Excavation Tourigny, and Glassers TV
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1491 (Defacement), T1657 (Financial Theft)

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian telecommunications/technology firms: Eeyou Communications Network and Solutions Extreme Technology
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1529 (System Shutdown/Reboot), T1489 (Service Stop)

items.name: Coinbase Cartel
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1020 (Automated Exfiltration)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Canada Goose (Consumer Services) - second attack on same organization
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service). Known data extortion group specializing in database theft.

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company - Database leaked (Financial Services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories). Active data breach with confirmed database exfiltration.

items.name: Anubis
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System), T1074 (Data Staged). Critical infrastructure healthcare targeting.

items.name: Worldleaks
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1114 (Email Collection). Municipal government targeting.

items.name: Rhysida
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1039 (Data from Network Shared Drive), T1560 (Archive Collected Data)

items.name: Akira
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021.001 (Remote Desktop Protocol), T1562.001 (Disable or Modify Tools)

items.name: Medusa
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol)

items.name: Lapsus$
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1528 (Steal Application Access Token), T1586 (Compromise Accounts). Known for social engineering and insider recruitment.

items.name: Payload
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery)

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: Regal Building Materials Ltd and one redacted Canadian firm
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1070 (Indicator Removal)

items.name: Kairos
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel)

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: John O's Foods (Agriculture and Food Production)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Securotrop
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1105 (Ingress Tool Transfer)

items.name: Sarcoma
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1561 (Disk Wipe)

items.name: Nitrogen
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

items.name: Devman
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: TridentLocker
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562 (Impair Defenses)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a coordinated ransomware crisis with 100+ organizations compromised across all provinces in 48 hours. Critical sectors under attack include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, Thunder Bay Counselling), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Collège Supérieur De Montréal), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health), and energy infrastructure. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period despite the severity of attacks.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose suffered dual ransomware attacks from both Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters groups. Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) and Kids & Company (childcare services) were also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a confirmed database leak by Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers was hit by Sinobi. Multiple accounting firms compromised: Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and SMT Lawyers.

report_2w.executive_summary: Fortinet FortiClientEMS is under active attack via CVE-2026-35616, a zero-day vulnerability confirmed by the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS). Canadian employees are being targeted in 'payroll pirate' attacks by the Storm-2755 group using poisoned Office 365 search results to steal paychecks. Iranian state-aligned hackers are conducting sophisticated attacks on critical infrastructure, including 5,219 exposed Rockwell PLCs, prompting an urgent CISA alert. Additionally, major supply chain attacks have compromised the Axios npm package, deploying RATs attributed to North Korean APT TA444/BlueNoroff, and the TeamPCP gang has breached the European Commission through poisoned software dependencies.

items.description: Zero-day vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClient EMS actively exploited in the wild
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway
items.software_affected: Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway

items.description: 13-year-old Apache ActiveMQ RCE bug uncovered by AI analysis
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Critical vulnerability impacting Axios library in AWS Lambda base images
items.software_affected: Axios npm package in AWS Lambda
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.name: Storm-2755 (Payroll Pirates)
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link via SEO poisoning), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information), credential theft via fake Office 365 login pages

items.name: Iranian APTs (State-aligned clusters)
items.targets: U.S. and Gulf critical infrastructure, Rockwell PLCs
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1098 (Account Manipulation), targeting ICS/SCADA systems, 5,219 exposed Rockwell PLCs

items.name: TA444 / BlueNoroff (North Korean APT)
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain), Axios npm package compromise, multi-platform RAT deployment

items.name: TeamPCP
items.targets: European Commission, software supply chain at large
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise), trojanized LiteLLM packages, dual C2 frameworks, 33K exposed deployments

items.name: Lazarus Group
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), fake job interviews, Graphalgo campaign since May 2025

items.name: MuddyWater (Iranian APT)
items.targets: Various sectors using Russian MaaS infrastructure
items.ttps: T1588.001 (Obtain Capabilities: Malware), ChainShell attack using Russian malware-as-a-service

items.name: LockBit 5.0
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), Ransomware-as-a-Service platform

items.name: Akira, Qilin, Sinobi (Ransomware gangs)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), accounted for 40% of ransomware attacks last month

items.name: Hive0117
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), DarkWatchman malware, fake payroll-themed attacks

items.name: Handala (Iranian-aligned group)
items.ttps: T1082 (System Information Discovery), hints at upcoming operation targeting Israel

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued two critical advisories: CVE-2026-35616 (Fortinet FortiClientEMS zero-day under active exploitation) and CVE-2026-3055 (Citrix NetScaler). Canadian employees are being heavily targeted by Storm-2755 'payroll pirate' attacks using poisoned Office 365 search results to steal credentials and redirect paychecks. No specific incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
